item.title: Hybrid Quantum Adjoint Method for PDE-Constrained Optimization
Markdown Syntax Guide
item.description: This article offers a sample of basic Markdown syntax that can be used in Hugo content files, also it shows whether basic HTML elements are decorated with CSS in a Hugo theme.

item.title: Rich Content
item.description: Hugo ships with several Built-in Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.

item.title: Placeholder Text
item.description: Lorem est tota propiore conpellat pectoribus de pectora summo.

item.title: Math Typesetting
item.description: Mathematical notation in a Hugo project can be enabled by using third party JavaScript libraries.

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description: Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways.

item.title: About
item.description:
Anish Ghosh

PhD Student, Applied Mathematics
University of Maryland, Baltimore County (UMBC)

I am currently a PhD student in the Matheamtics and Statistics Department at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County (UMBC). My work lies at the intersection of mathematical theory and computation, where rigorous analysis and modern computational methods inform one another.

My research interests include numerical analysis, partial differential equations, and optimization. I am particularly interested in studying the mathematical structure of difficult problems, developing theoretical understanding through analysis and proofs, and then using those insights to design more effective computational methods. I am interested in both the theoretical foundations—proofs, analysis, and algorithmic guarantees—and the practical implementation of scalable numerical methods.
